Platform & Data.narrative: This family ensures your customer information is accurate, accessible, and secure across all devices and touchpoints. Singapore SMEs need this to comply with PDPA regulations, enable remote and mobile teams, and integrate with existing tools like accounting software. A logistics company using WhatsApp to communicate with customers can log those interactions directly in CRM; a retail team can access customer history on their phones while on the shop floor.

Sales Transactions.narrative: This family handles the core mechanics of selling — from quoting and pricing through to invoicing and commission tracking. SMEs rely on this to process orders quickly, manage discounts without errors, and fairly compensate sales staff. A B2B distributor needs to quote different prices to different customers and track when those quotes convert; a small manufacturer needs to invoice promptly and track which rep earned commission on each deal.

CRM & Relationship.narrative: This family keeps track of every prospect and customer interaction, from first contact through to closed deal, so no lead falls through the cracks. SMEs benefit because it centralises relationship history, helps teams collaborate on accounts, and ensures consistent follow-up. A consulting firm can see all emails and calls with a prospect in one place before a pitch meeting; a sales team can hand off a lead to a colleague without losing context.

Marketing.narrative: This family automates customer communication and feedback collection across email, social media, and surveys to nurture prospects and retain customers. Singapore SMEs use this to stay top-of-mind with minimal manual effort, understand what customers think, and design targeted campaigns. A fintech startup can send automated onboarding emails to new users and collect NPS feedback; a fashion retailer can post to Instagram and Facebook from one inbox and see which posts drive engagement.

Commerce.narrative: This family powers online and omnichannel selling — from e-commerce storefronts and marketplace integration to loyalty programs and subscription billing. SMEs need this to reach customers beyond the physical store, manage inventory across channels, and build repeat revenue. A food supplier can sell on Shopee and Lazada from one product catalogue without manual syncing; a gym can manage memberships and recurring billing automatically.

Customer Service.narrative: This family organises customer support requests into a ticketing system with routing, SLA tracking, and knowledge base integration so issues are resolved faster and consistently. SMEs benefit by reducing response time, preventing tickets from being lost, and empowering staff to self-serve. A SaaS company can route support tickets by product and track whether they hit their 24-hour response target; a retailer can let customers find answers in a knowledge base before opening a ticket.

Analytics.narrative: This family turns raw CRM data into dashboards, forecasts, and AI-driven insights so leaders can see pipeline health, predict churn, and spot the next best action for each customer. SMEs use this to make faster, data-driven decisions and identify at-risk customers before they leave. A sales director can see which reps are on track to hit quota and which deals are stalling; a customer success team can predict which accounts are likely to churn and proactively intervene.
